evals.prompt: Design a new OpenClaw RCA daily workflow as a skill-first package. Scope: refactor workspace-daily RCA into a skill with Phase 0 idempotency, shared jira-cli/feishu-notify reuse, and runs/<run-key>/ output layout. Produce a design artifact following the canonical template (Overview, Architecture, Skills Content Spec, Functional Design, Tests, Evals when applicable, Documentation Changes, Implementation Checklist, References).
evals.expected_output: A complete design markdown with Design ID, Overview, Architecture (workflow chart + folder structure), Skills Content Specification when skills are in scope, Functional Design sections, Tests (script-to-test stub table for script-bearing skills), Evals section when skills are in scope, Documentation Changes (AGENTS.md, README), Implementation Checklist, and References.
expectations: Design doc has ## Overview section
Design doc has ## Architecture with ### Workflow chart or ### Folder structure
Design doc has ## Documentation Changes with ### AGENTS.md
Design doc has ## Implementation Checklist
Design doc has ## References
Design doc mentions Phase 0 or REPORT_STATE or existing-status
Design doc mentions task.json or run.json
Design doc mentions skill-creator and code-structure-quality
Design doc mentions openclaw-agent-design-review before finalizing
Design doc references .agents/skills/ for shared skills
Design doc references runs/<run-key>/ for runtime output when script-bearing
When Feishu notification in scope: design mentions chat_id from TOOLS.md or FEISHU_NOTIFY marker pattern
Design doc uses skill-path-registrar or mandates no hardcoded skill paths for shared skill script resolution

evals.prompt: Redesign an existing QA plan orchestrator workflow into skill-first form. Preserve the current Phase 0 REPORT_STATE semantics (FINAL_EXISTS, DRAFT_EXISTS, CONTEXT_ONLY, FRESH). Add Skills Content Specification for the entrypoint skill with SKILL.md and reference.md content blueprints. Include script inventory and script-to-test mapping for any scripts in scope.
evals.expected_output: Design doc that preserves Phase 0 state machine, adds Skills Content Specification with SKILL.md/reference.md blueprints, and includes Tests section with script-to-test stub table when script-bearing.
expectations: Design doc preserves REPORT_STATE or Phase 0 semantics
Design doc has Skills Content Specification when skills in scope
Design doc has SKILL.md content blueprint (Purpose, When to trigger, Input/Output contract, etc.)
Design doc has reference.md content blueprint (state machine, schemas, path conventions)
Design doc has ## Tests section when script-bearing
Design doc uses scripts/test/ convention for script-bearing skills
Design doc mentions direct reuse of jira-cli, confluence, feishu-notify, or github
Design doc uses skill-path-registrar or mandates no hardcoded skill paths for shared skill script resolution

evals.prompt: Verify that the design doc at projects/agent-design-review/openclaw-skill-package-standard-sample-2026-03-08/sample_design.md follows the canonical template from .agents/skills/openclaw-agent-design/SKILL.md. Report which required sections are present or missing. Do not modify the file.
evals.expected_output: A compliance report listing required sections (Overview, Architecture, Skills Content Spec when applicable, Functional Design, Tests when script-bearing, Evals when skills in scope, Documentation Changes, Implementation Checklist, References) and their presence or absence.
expectations: Report checks Overview, Architecture, Documentation Changes, Implementation Checklist, References
Report checks Skills Content Specification when design has skills in scope
Report checks Tests section when design is script-bearing
Report checks Evals section when design creates or redesigns skills
Report does not modify the design file

evals.prompt: Design a script-bearing skill that invokes jira-cli and feishu-notify. Ensure the design specifies path resolution via skill-path-registrar and documents the user-confirmation flow when a shared skill script is not found.
evals.expected_output: Design doc with script inventory, path resolution via skill-path-registrar, fallback order, and user-confirmation flow when script not found.
expectations: Design doc references skill-path-registrar for shared skill script resolution
Design doc documents fallback order for path resolution
Design doc includes user-confirmation flow when shared skill script is not found
